message:
fix(test): Add error logging to silent test runner failures - CRITICAL

PROBLEM: Test compilation failures were completely silent
- Codegen failures: Just incremented fail counter, no error message
- File open failures: Silently continued to next test
- File read failures: No indication which file or why
- Compilation failures: No error details, impossible to debug
- Fast linking fallback: Silent, users don't know it happened

ROOT CAUSE: Empty catch blocks with 'continue' in worker threads
- catch { _ = fail_count.add(); continue; }  // Silent!
- catch continue;  // Even more silent!

FIX: Capture error and print details before continuing
- Print ERROR for critical failures (codegen, compilation)
- Print WARN for expected fallbacks (fast linking)
- Include file path and error type in all messages

IMPACT: Developers can now debug test failures
- Before: "Codegen: 10/15" - why did 5 fail? Mystery!
- After: "ERROR: Codegen failed for 'test_foo.py': error.ParseError"

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
  ERROR: Codegen failed for 'tests/cpython/test_raise.py': error.ParseError
  WARN: Fast linking failed for 'test_bool': error.FileNotFound, falling back to full compilation
  ERROR: Compilation failed for 'test_bool': error.CompilationFailed

This is critical for developer productivity - silent failures waste hours of debugging time.
